semantic.role: Pipeline task for committing synchronized file state to SQLite after AST extraction and LLM summarization. Extends SpineTask to enforce stage-local execution within the ArchSpine pipeline.
responsibilities: Commits file hashes and metadata to the database after AST extraction and LLM summarization are complete.
Prevents write competition by ensuring partial failures during summarization do not leave the database in a 'synced' state for failed files.
Uses execution checkpointing to mark items as completed during the commit process, enabling resumability.
Reads pending file docs from the manifest and commits them in batches via ctx.manifest.commitBatch.
outOfScope: AST extraction or LLM summarization – this task runs after those stages.
CLI command parsing or general service orchestration.
Database schema management, migrations, or connection lifecycle.
File system operations beyond those exposed through the manifest API.
invariants: StateCommitTask executes only after both AST extraction and LLM summarization stages have completed.
It must not commit state for files that failed summarization – only files that passed the summarization stage are eligible.
All database writes are performed via the manifest interface (commitBatch, getFileDocs), preserving abstraction boundaries of the data layer.
The task honours checkpoint semantics: it can resume from a known state if interrupted.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Dedicate a well-defined pipeline stage for committing synchronized state to the database, maintaining the principle that each task works on top of core contracts and engines without assuming responsibility for other stages or cross-cutting concerns.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Initial commit establishing the state commitment task as part of the ArchSpine pipeline.

publicSurface.description: Pipeline task that commits file hashes and metadata to the database after AST extraction and LLM summarization. Called by the pipeline orchestrator to complete the sync cycle and persist the results of summarization.
